title: Origins of the Narkcoin Kingdom Quiz

questions.question: A new member asks whether narkcoin is a real cryptocurrency they can cash out. What's the accurate answer?

answers.text: Yes, it's backed by a small reserve the community set aside
answers.feedback: Narkcoin has no backing of any kind — it was explicitly built as the non-crypto, joke version of a coin.

answers.text: No — it's a fictional, zero-value in-joke currency that only exists inside Watercooler
answers.feedback: Correct. Narkcoin was created specifically because a real crypto scheme would violate community guidelines. It has no real-world value and can't be cashed out.

answers.text: Yes, but only admins can redeem it for real money
answers.feedback: There is no redemption mechanism for anyone, admins included. It's fictional by design.

answers.text: It's real, but only tradeable outside the WhatsApp group
answers.feedback: Narkcoin only exists within the bot's ledger inside Watercooler — there's nothing to trade elsewhere.

questions.question: Why is the entire narkcoin economy restricted to the Watercooler channel rather than the whole community?

answers.text: Watercooler is the only channel the bot can access
answers.feedback: Clawd operates across the whole community for moderation and assistance — the economy specifically is scoped to Watercooler by design.

answers.text: It's a deliberate boundary so the joke economy never leaks into channels where people are doing serious professional work
answers.feedback: Correct. Keeping narking, gambling, and the guillotine confined to Watercooler protects the serious channels (Business, Technical, Legal, etc.) from the bit.

answers.text: Other channels voted to opt out of it
answers.feedback: There's no vote involved — it's a structural design choice, not a preference members toggled off elsewhere.

answers.text: It was originally in every channel but got shut down everywhere except Watercooler after complaints
answers.feedback: It was designed to be Watercooler-only from the start, not rolled back after a wider rollout.

questions.question: Which of these are true about how Clawd rules on narks, fines, and appeals? (Select all that apply)

answers.text: Rulings are made using AI judgement in the moment, not a fixed lookup table of offences and penalties
answers.feedback: Correct. This is what gives the same kind of nark different outcomes depending on how it's argued.

answers.text: Clawd is exempt from being narked or fined itself
answers.feedback: The opposite is true — Clawd has its own wallet and is subject to the same narks, fines, and jokes as everyone else.

answers.text: Clawd's replies carry a distinct, consistent comedic personality
answers.feedback: Correct. Dry, self-aware, Kiwi-flavoured commentary is a core part of how rulings are delivered.

answers.text: Every fine amount is fixed in advance for each type of offence
answers.feedback: There's no fixed schedule — outcomes vary based on the AI's in-the-moment judgement of the specific case.

questions.question: A member loses an appeal to Karen and gets an extra fine on top for 'court costs.' Which outcome category does this describe?

answers.text: Upheld
answers.feedback: An upheld appeal reverses the original loss and refunds credits — the opposite of what happened here.

answers.text: Partially upheld
answers.feedback: Partially upheld means some credits are returned, not an additional fine on top of the original loss.

answers.text: Denied, with the appeal backfiring
answers.feedback: Correct. A backfiring appeal is denied and adds an extra penalty — Karen's harshest possible verdict.

answers.text: Automatic escalation to a community vote
answers.feedback: There's no voting mechanism in the appeals process — Karen alone rules on appeals.

questions.question: Match each character or concept to its role in the Narkcoin Kingdom:

answers.text: The Colab Clawd
answers.matchText: Treasury and referee — rules on narks, fines, and payouts

answers.text: Karen
answers.matchText: Chief Complaints Officer who presides over appeals

answers.text: Persona Roulette
answers.matchText: Hourly rotation of the bot's voice between straight Clawd and secret comedic personas

answers.text: The Keen Narks
answers.matchText: Nickname for the real community members who keep the economy running
